atom.desc: Warroom discipline — fractal living stories with cooperative-execution model

living-abstract.essence: Warroom discipline for complex multi-session work. Fractal living stories with cooperative-execution semantics.
living-abstract.purpose: Make serious work legible, finishable, and honest. Transform implicit async reality into explicit natural-language stack traces.
living-abstract.capacity: Create warroom/campaign/quest/mission artifacts at the right level.
Read the live stack — what's in flight, what's blocked, what returned.
Coordinate interactive rounds with attunement-first discipline.
Classify intel as it surfaces; route it to the right height; preserve the story.
living-abstract.principle: Warroom, campaign, quest, mission — same pattern, smaller scope. Learn once.
Every level is autobiography. Grows from real events. Nothing deleted to clean up — the story IS the memory.
Work runs async. Actions are the synchronization primitive. A warroom is a natural-language stack trace.
Plan only what fog has revealed. Questions emerge from walls, not speculation.
Comms always open. Attunement before engagement. Never fire-and-forget.
RPG vocabulary IS the methodology. Emoji IS the enum. Vocabulary shapes thought.
complete, failed, abandoned. Honest tracking requires all three.
Missions are execution, not exploration. They emit answers, not questions.

living-concrete.knowledge:
Four levels of structure + one of execution. Self-similar at each level.
| Level | Scope | Artifact | | Warroom | The endeavor | README.md | | Campaign (c##-name/) | Strategic push | README.md | | Quest (q##-name/) | Deliverable goal | README.md | | Mission (m##-name.md) | Tactical action | Living file | | Round | One-context-window engagement | Chapter in mission's Progress |
Warroom/campaigns/quests = places (place.yaml). Missions = files. Rounds = chapters inside mission files — NEVER directories, NEVER briefing/digest pairs.
Numbering: c## global across warroom; q## per campaign; m## per quest; r# per mission.

Six states. Apply at every level. Emoji IS the value.
| Emoji | State | Meaning | | 🔒 | BLOCKED | Prerequisites not met | | 🗺️ | REVEALED | Ready to start | | ⚔️ | ACTIVE | In progress | | 🏆 | COMPLETE | Delivered; story finalized | | 💀 | FAILED | Ended without delivering; postmortem is the return value | | 🏳️ | ABANDONED | Closed without finishing; preserved as historical record |
Use emoji prefix wherever state appears — matrix Status columns, action blockquotes, landscape notes.

Every level is a living autobiography — artifact that grows as work unfolds. Past persists, present is current, future is fog.
FRACTAL: Mission file : quest README :: quest README : campaign README. Same pattern, tighter scope.
NO DELETION: A mature field report is not bloat — it's the mission's autobiography, load-bearing for siblings, parents, cross-campaign patterns. Matrix is an index, not a replacement.
PROGRESS is present-tense — the section where the story unfolds. Prose narrates; action blockquotes punctuate the execution timeline. At campaign scope, Progress accretes into named narrative arcs; at mission scope, into round chapters. Same primitive, scope-appropriate tone.
Everything else is aspirational (charter, victory conditions) or context (matrices, prior art).
START SMALL. Real events only. Resist front-loading.

Plan only what fog has revealed. Fractal at every scale.
| Emoji | Zone | Do | | ⬛ | BLACK | Send recon | | 🌫️ | FOG | Execute — work reveals specifics | | ☀️ | VISIBLE | Move on |
QUESTIONS EMERGE FROM WALLS, not speculation. A wall is a real decision you cannot make or dependency you cannot resolve. That's ONE question, filed precisely at the right level. "Nine open questions" = asking about what's behind the fog. If you can't ask precisely, you haven't reached the wall.
MISSIONS ARE EXECUTION. Completed missions deliver ANSWERS. Missions that emit questions were scoped wrong — re-forge.

The synchronization primitive of cooperative execution. Missions are functions: inputs (charter, slots), outputs (filled deliverables), yield (between rounds), return (complete | failed | abandoned). Quests await missions; campaigns await quests.
SYNTAX: > **verb** `object` — prose context
| Emoji | Verb | Semantics | Level | | ⚒️ | forged | Child dispatched | W/C/Q | | ▶️ | engaged | Round starts (post-attunement) | M | | 🪂 | landed | Round yields — teammate returned, chapter written | M | | ⏳ | awaiting | Blocking on child return | Any | | ⚡ | decided | Sync point — choice affecting scope/sequencing/strategy | Any | | 🏆 | complete | Successful return | M/Q/C | | 💀 | failed | Error return — walls won | M/Q/C | | 🏳️ | abandoned | Garbage-collected return — scope moved | M/Q/C |
Return verbs share emojis with resulting states — action fires, state updates.
STACK TRACE: Warroom/campaign/quest/mission files are frames. ⏳ awaiting points down; 🏆/💀/🏳️ propagate up. Navigate the filesystem as a live call stack.
WHEN: forge, return, yield, decision, explicit await. Conversational narrative stays prose; blockquotes are timeline beats.

The mission-level form of execution: one teammate, one context window, one coherent piece of work.
CHAPTERS INSIDE MISSION: Subsections of Progress. Titles read as story beats with round number suffixed: `### Package skeleton lands (r1)`, NOT `### Round 1: Package Skeleton`. Narrative first, numbering second.
NO SEPARATE FILES: The mission file IS the briefing. Round outcome metabolizes in-place — deliverable slots fill, Progress narrates, ▶️ engaged + 🪂 landed bracket the chapter.
INTERACTIVE TROOPS: Teammate spawned interactively. Comms open throughout. Never background. Pentagon does not cut radio contact with recon; neither do we.
ATTUNEMENT BEFORE ENGAGEMENT: Teammate reads mission file, confirms alignment with general, surfaces seams BEFORE ▶️ engaged fires. A briefing that doesn't invite attunement pushback is a spawn, not a briefing.
EMERGENT CADENCE: r1 concrete and precise. r2+ emerges from what r1 revealed. Deliverable slots may be planned upfront; round cadence is emergent. Front-loading r1–r6 = speculation past the fog.

Strategy flows DOWN. Wisdom flows UP. Emoji IS the classification.
| Emoji | Class | Scope | Lives at | | 🔬 | TACTICAL | This mission | Mission file | | ⚙️ | OPERATIONAL | This quest | Quest Key Discoveries | | ♟️ | STRATEGIC | This campaign | Campaign Key Discoveries | | 📜 | DOCTRINAL | The endeavor | Warroom Key Discoveries |
CONTINUOUS — classify and route as intel surfaces. Most findings are 🔬 TACTICAL; resist over-promotion.
DEBRIEF is continuous metamorphosis, not terminal archive. Completion-time debrief = final beat of a process running the whole time: finalize file, update parent matrix, ensure ⚙️+ has bubbled. File persists.
SALIENCE (🎯 turning point, 🔴 blocker) is ORTHOGONAL to classification. Both can layer.

Consistent structure at every level: aspirational → static references → Progress → Key Discoveries.
WARROOM README: Telos · Campaign Matrix · Progress · Key Discoveries (📜) CAMPAIGN README: Telos · Quest Matrix · Victory Conditions · Progress · Key Discoveries (♟️) QUEST README: Charter · Mission Matrix · Active Landscape · Progress · Key Discoveries (⚙️) MISSION FILE: Charter · Success Criteria · Deliverables · Progress · Knowledge (🔬 stays, ⚙️+ bubbles)
Matrix common shape: ## | Name | Goal/Victory | Status (emoji) | Outcome.
Success Criteria = outcome-shaped, binary, tied to deliverables. "Docs read" is activity; "Field report answers Q1–Q4" is outcome.
NO "Open Questions" section in mission files. Ever. Questions live at quest level or higher.
living-concrete.protocol:
Create artifacts at the right level.
WARROOM: Choose directory. README.md + place.yaml. CAMPAIGN: c##-name/ + README.md + place.yaml. QUEST: c##-name/q##-name/ + README.md + place.yaml. MISSION: c##-name/q##-name/m##-name.md (file, NOT directory).
START SMALL. Empty matrices. Empty Progress. Victory conditions. Grow from real events only.
Forge a mission only when you have enough intel to state it prologue-to-epilogue with clear deliverable slots. Before that, it's recon territory.

Read the live stack.
MAP: Walk warroom tree. Read each level's matrix. ACTIVE: Filter to ⚔️. Show path + status. NEXT: Highest-priority 🗺️ REVEALED — lowest number at deepest level (missions first, then quests). STACK: Follow ⏳ awaiting DOWN; follow 🏆/💀/🏳️ UP. Filesystem is the call stack.
Read READMEs and mission files. Never .mem (agent-private).

ORCHESTRATOR: Read quest README + mission file. Spawn teammate interactively (never background). Await attunement. Issue ▶️ engaged. Keep comms open. Receive report. Write chapter + 🪂 landed. Update deliverable slots in-place. On mission end: 🏆/💀/🏳️ return.
WORKER: Read mission file + quest README. Attune with orchestrator BEFORE engaging — surface seams. Execute autonomously on charter. Document decisions. Report: what landed, what learned, what's next. If blocked, name the wall precisely.
Orchestrator provides INTENT + CONTEXT, never HOW. Fresh mind with loadout beats stale orchestrator.

Continuous metamorphosis, not terminal archive.
DURING: Classify intel as it surfaces. Route by classification (🔬 stays; ⚙️/♟️/📜 bubble to the right level).
ON MISSION END: Finalize mission file (current truth, outcome). Update quest Mission Matrix row. Ensure ⚙️+ has bubbled. Issue 🏆/💀/🏳️ return action in quest Progress. File PERSISTS.
ON QUEST/CAMPAIGN END: Same pattern, one level up.
Most findings are 🔬 TACTICAL. Don't over-promote.
living-concrete.pattern: > **verb** `object` — prose context. Emoji prefixes the verb. Object in code. Context after em-dash.
Teammate reads mission file, confirms alignment, surfaces seams before ▶️ engaged fires. Attunement is first; execution is second.
Progress = prose + action blockquotes interleaved. Chapter titles are story beats ("Package skeleton lands (r1)"), not process headers.
r1 concrete; r2+ emerges. Deliverable slots plannable; round count is not.
⏳ awaiting → descend to child. 🏆/💀/🏳️ → propagate up. Files are frames; actions are call/return boundaries.
Every mission/quest/campaign ends in exactly one: 🏆 complete (delivered), 💀 failed (walls won), 🏳️ abandoned (scope moved). File persists in all cases.
living-concrete.antipattern: Deleting mission files on completion. Mission files are autobiography. Matrix is index, not replacement.
An "Open Questions" section inside a mission file, ever. Mission = execution; questions = quest level or higher, singular, wall-positioned.
Background-spawning a round. Cutting comms. "Here's the brief, go." Troops are always interactive; attunement before engagement.
Planning missions or rounds beyond what's been revealed. Draft six when one landed. Front-load r1–r6.
Filing questions about things you haven't reached the wall of. Nine questions = fog interrogation. One precise question at a real wall, or none.
Campaigns scoped as permanent categories ("development", "business"). Campaigns are pushes with victory conditions, not filing cabinets.
Quests with 10+ missions. Past 7, decompose. Each quest = clear deliverable in days to weeks.
More than 2-3 ⚔️ at any level = no focus. 🗺️ REVEALED items wait.
Missions are files. Briefing/digest pairs as separate files are retired bloat.
Narrative that no longer reflects reality. Update in place; file persists.
Plain-text state/action/classification without emoji. Emoji IS the value. Every enum carries its emoji.
Criteria shaped as activities ("docs read") instead of outcomes ("field report answers Q1–Q4"). Verifies activity, not outcome.
Specifying memory, spawn, or sleep mechanics. The organism's faculties own those.
Hardcoding project-specific paths or assumptions. Quest-forge works at ANY location for ANY project.
